title: School district air-quality alerts and classroom mitigation claims
public_interest_reason: Families need clear, evidence-bound communication when public agencies claim that classrooms are safe during an air-quality alert.
summary: A fictional school district said classrooms were safe during a smoke-related air-quality alert.
Parent and teacher observations later challenged whether all classrooms had working filtration and timely notifications.
A follow-up district update acknowledged equipment gaps and promised room-level checks.

source_fixtures.title: District says schools remain open with air filtration active
source_fixtures.draft_text: The district announced that schools would remain open because central filtration was running and classroom air quality was being monitored.

source_fixtures.title: Families report inconsistent classroom air filtering and late alerts
source_fixtures.draft_text: Parents and teachers reported several classrooms with missing portable filters, delayed notification emails, and uncertainty about whether outdoor activities were cancelled.

source_fixtures.title: District follow-up says filters and room checks were added
source_fixtures.draft_text: The district issued a follow-up saying it had added portable filters to affected rooms, corrected notification lists, and started room-level checks.

expected_facts.text: The district said schools would remain open while filtration systems were running.
Parent and teacher observations described missing portable filters in some classrooms.
The district follow-up said portable filters and room-level checks were added.

expected_actor_claims.claim_text: Classrooms were safe for in-person school during the air-quality alert.
Some classrooms lacked working filtration and families did not receive timely alerts.

expected_actions.action_text: Added portable filters to affected rooms and corrected notification lists.

expected_interpretations.title: Communication and implementation gap in school air-quality response
expected_interpretations.interpretation_text: The initial safety assurance may have been too broad for room-level conditions until the district corrected equipment and notification gaps.
alternative_interpretations: Some gaps may reflect fast-changing conditions rather than misleading communication.

expected_counter_branches.title: Rapid smoke conditions and equipment logistics may explain part of the gap
expected_counter_branches.counter_text: The district follow-up suggests partial remediation and may indicate operational lag rather than intentional misrepresentation.
expected_counter_branches.what_would_change_this: Room-level air readings, filter deployment logs, and notification timestamps before and after the update.

expected_bias_notes.note_text: Community reports may use urgent safety language that should be separated from source-bound evidence.

expected_version_timeline.summary: The district said classrooms were safe while filtration was active.
expected_version_timeline.judgment_at_version: Adequate on paper; room-level conditions unknown.
open_questions: Were all classrooms actually filtered and monitored?

expected_version_timeline.summary: Observations challenged the broad safety claim and the district added filters and room checks.
open_questions: Did remediation reach affected classrooms before exposure risk increased?

expected_claim_drift.drift_summary: The broad classroom safety claim weakened after room-level observations reported missing filtration.
expected_claim_drift.current_handling: Keep the district claim as an initial assurance, but mark it as partially challenged.

expected_version_diff.previous_judgment: The district claim sounded adequate, but classroom-level implementation was unknown.
expected_version_diff.updated_judgment: Concern shifted to a partially corrected communication and implementation gap.
unchanged_uncertainties: Whether room-level air readings supported the district's safety assurance.
Whether families received alerts early enough to make decisions.

expected_editorial_verdict.short_label: School air-quality communication gap with partial remediation
expected_editorial_verdict.verdict_text: This draft should be treated as a school safety communication and implementation-gap scenario until room-level evidence is added.
reader_warnings: Draft authoring template, not a validated demo scenario.
No independent verification has been performed.

authoring_notes: Promote this template only after all facts, claims, actions, and drift entries are evidence-bound.
Build a claim_graph only after stable IDs are assigned.
